text: Diamonds are made of carbon arranged in a very strong crystal structure, making them extremely durable. Under normal conditions, diamonds can last billions of years—they are effectively "forever" on a human timescale. However, under extremely high temperatures (around 700–900°C in the presence of oxygen), diamonds can slowly turn into graphite, but this process takes a very long time and doesn’t occur under typical environmental conditions. So, for all practical purposes, diamonds last indefinitely.
